title: selaginella aboriginalis
intro: Selaginella aboriginalis, also known as the Black Bean or Queensland Spikemoss, is a species of spikemoss that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Aboriginal medicine in Australia for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Selaginella aboriginalis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in treating various health conditions such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of a tea or infusion, or as a topical application, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
